repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

0 and `nil` progress percent from nvim_echo in Progress autocommands have the same behavior

## Solution

Distinguish 0 and `nil` progress percent so that they can be differentiated in Progress autocommands
As a result, in the default tui Progress autocommand, `nil` emits a indeterminate progress OSC 9 sequence,
while allowing 0 to emit zero progress.
Additionally, statusline percent now shows 0% when previously it was omitted.

## Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d27ff6fa-1351-43d8-b95f-79326523ca1c

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1107,7 +1107,11 @@ do
       desc = 'Display native progress bars',
       callback = function(ev)
         if ev.data.status == 'running' then
-          vim.api.nvim_ui_send(string.format('\027]9;4;1;%d\027\\', ev.data.percent))
+          if ev.data.percent then
+            vim.api.nvim_ui_send(string.format('\027]9;4;1;%d\027\\', ev.data.percent))
+          else
+            vim.api.nvim_ui_send(string.format('\027]9;4;3\027\\'))

@@ -3600,6 +3675,11 @@ describe('progress-message', function()
       t.pcall_err(api.nvim_echo, { { 'test-message' } }, false, { percent = 10 })
     )
 
+    eq(
+      "Conflict: title/source/status/percent/data not allowed with kind='echo'",
+      t.pcall_err(api.nvim_echo, { { 'test-message' } }, false, { percent = 0 })

comment_data.body: this is a lot of code that, AFAICT, is just testing that the `percent` field is passed through. if i'm wrong please point out where. if not let's drop this. we already have test coverage for nvim_echo.

well, _slight_ distinction, i wanted to test that a `nil` percent field was _not_ passed through (which would fail without this pr). but this distinction seems pretty small related to what you're concerned about so i'll remove
i changed the above test to be 0 to sorta kill two birds with one stone
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -3600,6 +3675,11 @@ describe('progress-message', function()
       t.pcall_err(api.nvim_echo, { { 'test-message' } }, false, { percent = 10 })
     )
 
+    eq(
+      "Conflict: title/source/status/percent/data not allowed with kind='echo'",
+      t.pcall_err(api.nvim_echo, { { 'test-message' } }, false, { percent = 0 })

comment_data.body: maybe that could be added to an existing test

comment_regular_data.body:
>Treat percent either as 0 or unset as indeterminate.

Is this what applications actually do? I wouldn't expect 0 to have this behavior. `0` is 0%, not indeterminate. Interpreting `percent == nil` as "indeterminate" makes sense though.
hmm i was trying to figure out a way to do that, let me dig deeper
@gpanders used ai to help find edit the right code here, would appreciate another look 🙏
sorry, seems like ci is failing legitimately, looking

ok i fixed one of the errors, but wasn't able to reproduce errors currently in CI locally and they seem unrelated. pr has become bigger than i initially had it, so let me know if it's acceptable in this state. also not sure if this should be considered a breaking change since we did have failures here
please update the pr description / commit message to be more accurate since the revisions
@justinmk updated pr, would appreciate another look!

title: feat(api): distinguish 0 from `nil` progress percent in nvim_echo
